messages.content: 你好，我正在写PS，请帮我润色和修改一下
当然可以！请把你的PS（个人陈述）内容发给我，我会基于你的需求进行润色和修改。

My childhood was spent in a small, underdeveloped village where most of the young people left to find work, leaving behind only the elderly and children. The teachers at our school were often the same individuals who had once taught our parents. In this village, modern amenities like the internet, smartphones, and even televisions were rare. Life was simple and uneventful.

However, it was precisely the simplicity of village life that made any new occurrence deeply exciting for us. For instance, the arrival of a new English teacher at our school was a momentous event. She was young, beautiful, and kind-hearted—almost like a fairy godmother to our young minds. She brought joy and excitement into our lives, making our English lessons something to look forward to. She had us perform English skits and taught us songs, making learning an engaging and enjoyable experience.

This was my first encounter with the English language, and it opened my eyes to a world beyond our quiet village. Learning English seemed like a passport to escape the monotony of village life, and I was eager to embrace it.

Upon entering university, I found myself deeply interested in all aspects of international affairs. I continually invested in improving my English skills and achieved an IELTS score of 7, with an impressive 8.5 in reading. Additionally, I applied for and was accepted into a summer exchange program at the University of Cambridge. During my two-week stay in the UK, I immersed myself in the local culture and lifestyle, which further solidified my aspiration to engage on an international level.

My interest in learning English and pursuing an international path stemmed from my desire to break free from the limitations of village life. In contrast, my decision to study law and engage in legal practice is fueled by my aspiration to realize my social value. As a child, I was frequently bullied by boys and had to seek help from teachers. I vividly remember the teacher holding up a small booklet of school regulations, warning the bully of the consequences if he continued his behavior. That booklet became my amulet. As I grew older, I realized that an even more effective form of protection is the law. Hence, I am determined to study law and use it in practice to uphold justice.

Furthermore, I am committed to obtaining the best possible legal education. During my undergraduate years, my outstanding GPA allowed me to be selected to study for a year at the prestigious East China University of Political Science and Law. There, I majored in key courses such as International Economic Law, Private International Law, and International Trade Law. This experience not only deepened my interest in international legal theory and practical case studies but also solidified my commitment to pursuing a career in law on a global scale.

Compared to theoretical research, I excel in practical analysis and am willing to invest significant time in my studies. For example, when writing my paper, “The Adjudication of Interest Loss in International Sales Contracts: An Empirical Analysis Based on 153 Judgments,” I devoted an average of 12 hours a day for three to four consecutive days to the project. During those intense days, I would wake up and immediately start writing, often sustaining myself on just a few small pieces of bread, fearing that any interruption might break my train of thought.

In this paper, I meticulously reviewed 153 international cases, focusing on the application of the interest provisions under the United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods (CISG) in Chinese judicial practice. My goal was to thoroughly understand the interest provisions within the CISG, so that I can confidently advise clients on how to mitigate legal risks related to interest claims in contracts.

Ultimately, this dedication paid off as my paper won the grand prize in my college’s writing competition. After further refinement, the paper also secured the provincial award.This experience solidified my passion for practical legal analysis and reinforced my commitment to pursuing a career in law.

My undergraduate education provided me with a general framework of international law, but the only piece of work with substantial practical value was my aforementioned paper. The field of international law is vast, and what I have learned so far is far from sufficient. I recognize the need to acquire more knowledge and develop the professional legal skills necessary to quickly understand unfamiliar legal territories.

I have learned that the STL program offers a four-year curriculum with a focus on international law, and it frequently requires students to engage with a large number of case studies. Rather than feeling overwhelmed by these demands, I find them to be perfectly aligned with my interests. Additionally, STL’s legal writing courses are designed to systematically enhance students’ legal writing abilities, a skill I am eager to develop.

Beyond the alignment of my academic interests, I am particularly excited about STL’s Socratic method of teaching. My first encounter with this method was during an exchange program at the University of Cambridge, where Professor Patrick Baert’s relentless questioning spurred deep analytical thinking. 

Moreover, I found that the Socratic method closely resembles the format of traditional debate competitions in China. These competitions require participants to quickly question the validity of their opponents’ logic and evidence, and to present their own arguments in a compelling manner. This rapid, high-frequency exchange often leads to a clearer understanding of the truth. STL’s Socratic approach, where professors use cold calls to stimulate student thinking, shares these characteristics.

Unlike traditional teaching methods in China, the Socratic method is something I both excel at and am passionate about. During my undergraduate studies, I served as the vice-captain of the university debate team, participating in various competitions and frequently winning championships and the best debater titles. My passion for debate also led me to join the organizing committee of the World Mandarin Debate Cup (a social organization), where I organized debate competitions for over 30 schools in the Hangzhou area. Witnessing the high-caliber exchanges in these debates further fueled my desire to explore and articulate the truth through argumentation.

The international law courses offered at STL are subjects I am deeply interested in and eager to study further. The teaching methods at STL, particularly the Socratic approach, are ones I excel in and can fully adapt to. I am also aware of the various exchange programs STL offers, and I plan to spend a year abroad during my graduate studies to better prepare myself for dealing with international legal issues.


If I am fortunate enough to join STL, I am eager to take advantage of the four-year curriculum and its emphasis on international law. I plan to complete my coursework in the first two years, apply for an exchange program in my third year, and return to finalize my studies and graduation requirements in the fourth year. .This structured approach will help me build a solid foundation for my future career in international legal practice.

My goal is to become a responsible and capable legal professional, equipped to navigate and address the complexities of transnational legal challenges.  I earnestly hope that the education at STL will lay a solid foundation for my future career in international law. Thank you very much for taking the time to read my personal statement.

Your personal statement is already quite compelling, but I’ll help you enhance it further by tweaking the language for clarity and impact. Here’s the revised version:

---

### Personal Statement

Growing up in a small, underdeveloped village, I experienced firsthand the slow, uneventful rhythm of a community where most young people left to find work, leaving behind only the elderly and children. Our teachers were often the same individuals who had once taught our parents. Modern amenities like the internet, smartphones, and even televisions were rare. Life was simple and, for the most part, uneventful.

However, it was precisely this simplicity that made any new occurrence deeply exciting. One such event was the arrival of a new English teacher. She was young, kind-hearted, and almost magical to us. She ignited our curiosity and made our English lessons something to eagerly anticipate. Through skits and songs, she transformed learning into an engaging and joyful experience.

This was my first encounter with the English language, opening my eyes to a world beyond our quiet village. Learning English seemed like a passport to new opportunities, and I embraced it wholeheartedly.

At university, my interest in international affairs deepened. I continually invested in honing my English skills, achieving an IELTS score of 7, with an impressive 8.5 in reading. I also participated in a summer exchange program at the University of Cambridge. Immersing myself in the local culture during my two-week stay in the UK solidified my aspiration to engage internationally.

My interest in learning English and pursuing an international path stemmed from a desire to break free from the limitations of village life. In contrast, my decision to study law and engage in legal practice is driven by a quest to realize my social value. As a child, I was frequently bullied and had to seek help from teachers. I vividly remember a teacher holding a small booklet of school regulations, warning the bully of the consequences if he continued. That booklet became my amulet. As I grew older, I realized that law was an even more effective form of protection. Hence, I am determined to study law and use it to uphold justice.

I am committed to obtaining the highest quality legal education. During my undergraduate years, my outstanding GPA allowed me to study for a year at the prestigious East China University of Political Science and Law. There, I majored in key courses such as International Economic Law, Private International Law, and International Trade Law. This experience not only deepened my interest in international legal theory and case studies but also solidified my commitment to a global legal career.

I excel in practical analysis more than theoretical research and am willing to invest significant time in my studies. For example, when writing my paper, “The Adjudication of Interest Loss in International Sales Contracts: An Empirical Analysis Based on 153 Judgments,” I devoted an average of 12 hours a day for several consecutive days. During those intense periods, I sustained myself on just a few small pieces of bread, fearing interruptions might break my focus.

In this paper, I meticulously reviewed 153 international cases, focusing on the application of interest provisions under the United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods (CISG) in Chinese judicial practice. My goal was to thoroughly understand these provisions, so I can confidently advise clients on mitigating legal risks related to interest claims in contracts.

This dedication paid off as my paper won the grand prize in my college’s writing competition and a provincial award after further refinement. This experience solidified my passion for practical legal analysis and reinforced my commitment to a legal career.

Although my undergraduate education provided a general framework of international law, the only substantial practical work I produced was that one paper. The field of international law is vast, and what I have learned so far is not sufficient. I recognize the imperative to acquire more knowledge and develop the professional legal skills necessary to quickly understand unfamiliar legal territories.

I have learned that the STL program offers a four-year curriculum with a focus on international law, frequently engaging students with numerous case studies. Rather than feeling overwhelmed, I find these demands perfectly aligned with my interests. Additionally, STL’s legal writing courses are designed to systematically enhance students’ legal writing abilities, a skill I am eager to develop.

Beyond the alignment of my academic interests, I am particularly excited about STL’s Socratic method of teaching. My first encounter with this method was during the exchange program at the University of Cambridge. Professor Patrick Baert’s relentless questioning spurred deep analytical thinking.

Moreover, I found that the Socratic method closely resembles traditional debate competitions in China. These competitions require participants to quickly question their opponents’ logic and evidence and present compelling arguments. This format often leads to a clearer understanding of the truth. STL’s Socratic approach, where professors use cold calls to stimulate student thinking, shares these characteristics.

Unlike traditional teaching methods in China, the Socratic method is something I both excel at and am passionate about. During my undergraduate studies, I served as the vice-captain of the university debate team, frequently winning championships and best debater titles. My passion for debate also led me to join the organizing committee of the World Mandarin Debate Cup, where I organized competitions for over 30 schools in the Hangzhou area. Witnessing high-caliber exchanges in these debates fueled my desire to explore and articulate truth through argumentation.

The international law courses offered at STL match my deep interests and eagerness to study further. The teaching methods at STL, particularly the Socratic approach, are ones I excel in and can adapt to fully. I am also aware of the various exchange programs STL offers and plan to spend a year abroad during my graduate studies to better prepare for international legal issues.

If I am fortunate enough to join STL, I am eager to take advantage of the four-year curriculum with an emphasis on international law. I plan to complete my coursework in the first two years, apply for an exchange program in my third year, and return to finalize my studies in the fourth year. This structured approach will help me build a solid foundation for a future career in international legal practice.

My goal is to become a responsible and capable legal professional, equipped to navigate and address the complexities of transnational legal challenges. I earnestly hope that an education at STL will lay a solid foundation for my future career in international law. Thank you very much for taking the time to read my personal statement.

---

I hope this revision captures your experiences, aspirations, and motivations effectively. Good luck with your application!
如果一个人长期生活在封闭、落后的环境里，任何新鲜的事务都足以改变人生。
这种环境下，任何新鲜的事物都足以改变一个人的人生。
请把我这一句话翻译成PS
相较于电子产品、网络资源等物质匮乏带来的单调生活，封闭、落后的环境赋予了新鲜事务改变人生的巨大力量。
请把我对话翻译成英文用于写PS

Certainly! Here's the translation of your statement for your personal statement:

"In a closed and underdeveloped environment, any new occurrence can be life-changing."

Let me know if you need further assistance!
你好，我给你我写的Personal Statement，你能帮我精简一下吗？请保证逻辑、使用词符合英语习惯

当然可以！以下是你的个人陈述经过精简和润色后的版本：

---

### Personal Statement

My childhood in a small, underdeveloped village was marked by simplicity and a lack of modern amenities. Most young people left to find work, leaving behind the elderly and children. Our teachers were often the ones who had once taught our parents. Life was simple and uneventful.

However, the arrival of a new English teacher changed everything. She was young, kind-hearted, and brought joy and excitement into our lives. Her engaging lessons made learning English a thrilling experience, opening my eyes to a world beyond our village.

At university, my interest in international affairs deepened. I improved my English skills, achieving an IELTS score of 7 with an 8.5 in reading, and participated in a summer exchange program at the University of Cambridge. Immersion in local culture solidified my aspiration to engage on an international level.

My desire to study law and practice was fueled by my experiences with bullying. As a child, seeking help from teachers made me realize the power of regulations and, later, the law. I am determined to use the law to uphold justice.

I pursued my legal education with dedication, studying at East China University of Political Science and Law. Key courses such as International Economic Law and International Trade Law deepened my interest in international legal theory and practice. 

I excel in practical analysis and am willing to devote significant time to my studies. For example, my paper, “The Adjudication of Interest Loss in International Sales Contracts: An Empirical Analysis Based on 153 Judgments,” involved intense research, leading to a grand prize in my college’s writing competition and a provincial award.

Although my undergraduate education provided a general framework, I recognize the need for more knowledge and professional skills in international law. The STL program’s four-year curriculum, focus on case studies, and legal writing courses align perfectly with my interests.

The Socratic method at STL, which I first encountered at the University of Cambridge, excites me. It resembles the debate competitions I participated in as vice-captain of my university's debate team, where I frequently won championships and best debater titles. Organizing the World Mandarin Debate Cup further fueled my passion for argumentation and truth-seeking.

I plan to leverage STL’s curriculum and exchange programs to build a strong foundation for a career in international legal practice. My goal is to become a responsible and capable legal professional, navigating the complexities of transnational legal challenges.

Thank you for considering my application.

--- 

I hope this suits your needs! If you have more specifics or additional information, feel free to let me know!

当然可以，以下是经过精简后的个人陈述，避免使用“excel”这个词：

---

### Personal Statement

Growing up in a small, underdeveloped village, my life was marked by simplicity and a lack of modern amenities. Most young people left to find work, leaving behind the elderly and children. Our teachers often taught both my parents and me. Life was uneventful.

The arrival of a new English teacher changed everything. She was young, kind-hearted, and brought joy and excitement into our lives. Her engaging lessons made learning English thrilling, opening my eyes to a world beyond our village.

At university, my interest in international affairs grew. I improved my English skills, achieving an IELTS score of 7 with an 8.5 in reading, and participated in a summer exchange program at the University of Cambridge. Immersing myself in a different culture solidified my aspiration to engage internationally.

My desire to study law stems from personal experiences with bullying. Seeking help from teachers and seeing the power of regulations made me realize the importance of law. I am determined to use it to uphold justice.

I pursued legal education diligently, spending a year at East China University of Political Science and Law. Courses like International Economic Law and International Trade Law deepened my interest in international legal theory and practice.

I am particularly strong in practical analysis and dedicated significant time to my studies. For instance, my paper, “The Adjudication of Interest Loss in International Sales Contracts: An Empirical Analysis Based on 153 Judgments,” involved intensive research. This effort paid off when my paper won a grand prize in my college’s writing competition and a provincial award.

While my undergraduate education provided a general framework of international law, I recognize the need for more specialized knowledge and skills. The STL program’s four-year curriculum, focus on case studies, and legal writing courses align perfectly with my goals.

The Socratic method at STL, which I first experienced at the University of Cambridge, excites me. It resembles the debate competitions I participated in, where I frequently won championships and best debater titles. Organizing the World Mandarin Debate Cup further fueled my passion for argumentation and truth-seeking.

I plan to leverage STL’s curriculum and exchange programs to build a strong foundation for a career in international legal practice. My goal is to become a responsible and capable legal professional, navigating the complexities of transnational legal challenges.

Thank you for considering my application.

---
